sentences.text: Det er min første dag i København.
sentences.translation: It's my first day in Copenhagen.

words.grammar: første · ordinal of én (one)

sentences.text: Jeg står på hovedbanegården og ser mig omkring.
sentences.translation: I'm standing at the main train station and looking around.

words.grammar: hoved (main) + banegård (station) + -en (the)
se sig omkring = to look around

sentences.text: Solen skinner og det er varmt.
sentences.translation: The sun is shining and it's warm.

words.grammar: en sol (a sun) · solen (the sun) · -en suffix = definite

sentences.text: Jeg har et kort i hånden.
sentences.translation: I have a map in my hand.

words.grammar: et kort · neuter noun · also means 'short' as adj.
en hånd (a hand) · hånden (the hand)

sentences.text: Først vil jeg se Nyhavn.
sentences.translation: First I want to see Nyhavn.

words.grammar: ny (new) + havn (harbor)

sentences.text: Himlen er blå i dag.
sentences.translation: The sky is blue today.

words.grammar: en himmel (a sky) · himlen (the sky) · -el drops before -en
blå · same form for all genders and plural

sentences.text: Jeg går langs kanalen og ser de farverige huse.
sentences.translation: I walk along the canal and see the colorful houses.

words.grammar: en kanal · kanalen (the canal)
farverig · farve (color) + -rig (-ful) · plural: farverige

sentences.text: København er en smuk by.
sentences.translation: Copenhagen is a beautiful city.

words.grammar: smuk · comparative: smukkere · superlative: smukkest
en by · byen (the city) · byer (cities)

sentences.text: Om aftenen spiser jeg smørrebrød.
sentences.translation: In the evening I eat smørrebrød (open-faced sandwiches).

words.grammar: en aften · aftenen (the evening) · om aftenen = in the evening
smør (butter) + brød (bread) · iconic Danish food

sentences.text: Min første dag var dejlig.
sentences.translation: My first day was lovely.

words.grammar: dejlig · one of the most-used Danish positive adjectives

sentences.text: Pippi bor alene i et gammelt hus.
sentences.translation: Pippi lives alone in an old house.

words.grammar: bo (to live/reside) · bor (present) · boede (past) · a core everyday verb, distinct from leve (to be alive)
gammel · et gammelt hus (neuter) · en gammel mand (common) · irregular: gammel – gammelt – gamle

sentences.text: Huset hedder Villakulla.
sentences.translation: The house is called Villakulla.

words.grammar: hedde (to be called) · hedder (present) · hed (past) · Hvad hedder du? = What is your name?
et hus · huset (the house) · neuter noun · husene (the houses, pl. definite)

sentences.text: Pippi har en abe og en hest.
sentences.translation: Pippi has a monkey and a horse.

words.grammar: en abe · aben (the monkey) · aber (monkeys) · aberne (the monkeys)
en hest · hesten (the horse) · heste (horses) · common gender noun

sentences.text: Hesten bor på verandaen.
sentences.translation: The horse lives on the veranda.

words.grammar: en veranda · verandaen (the veranda) · borrowed word, always common gender

sentences.text: Naboerne hedder Tommy og Annika.
sentences.translation: The neighbours are called Tommy and Annika.

words.grammar: en nabo · naboen (the neighbour) · naboer (neighbours) · naboerne (the neighbours)

sentences.text: De ser Pippi og bliver meget overraskede.
sentences.translation: They see Pippi and become very surprised.

words.grammar: overrasket (sg.) · overraskede (pl./definite) · blive overrasket = to be/become surprised · en overraskelse = a surprise
blive (to become/stay) · bliver (present) · blev (past) · very versatile: blive glad = to become happy · blive hjemme = to stay home

sentences.text: Pippi siger: "Jeg bor her nu. Vil I være mine venner?"
sentences.translation: Pippi says: 'I live here now. Will you be my friends?'

words.grammar: en ven (m.) · venner (friends, pl.) · en veninde = female friend · gode venner = good friends
ville (will/want) · I (you plural) · Vil I? = Do you (all) want to? · I is the pl. subject pronoun (informal): rarely used in modern speech, but correct

sentences.text: Tommy og Annika siger ja.
sentences.translation: Tommy and Annika say yes.

words.grammar: sige (to say) · siger (present) · sagde (past) · sagt (past participle) · Hvad siger du? = What do you say?

sentences.text: Pippi laver mad til dem begge.
sentences.translation: Pippi makes food for both of them.

words.grammar: lave (to make/do) · laver mad = to cook/make food · mad (c.) = food · laver du mad? = do you cook?
begge = both · begge to = both of them · used with plural nouns · ikke begge = not both

sentences.text: Hun er stærk og sjov – den stærkeste pige i verden!
sentences.translation: She is strong and funny – the strongest girl in the world!

words.grammar: stærk · stærkere (comparative) · stærkest/den stærkeste (superlative) · en stærk vilje = a strong will
sjov (common) · sjovt (neuter) · sjove (pl./definite) · det er sjovt = it's fun · have det sjovt = to have fun
en verden · verden (the world – already definite) · i hele verden = in the whole world

sentences.text: Pippi vil gå på markedet i dag.
sentences.translation: Pippi wants to go to the market today.

words.grammar: et marked · markedet (the market) · markeder (markets) · marked can also mean 'marked/noticed'
ville (to want/will) · vil (present) · ville (past) · Jeg vil gerne = I would like to · modal verb

sentences.text: Hun tager en stor kurv med sig.
sentences.translation: She takes a large basket with her.

words.grammar: en kurv · kurven (the basket) · kurve (baskets) · also used idiomatically: få en kurv = to be rejected (romantically)
tage med (to take along) · tager ... med · tog med (past) · Må jeg tage det med? = May I take this?

sentences.text: På markedet er der mange farver og lyde.
sentences.translation: At the market there are many colours and sounds.

words.grammar: en farve · farven (the colour) · farver (colours) · hvad er din yndlingsfarve? = what is your favourite colour?
en lyd · lyden (the sound) · lyde (sounds) · høre en lyd = to hear a sound

sentences.text: Pippi køber æbler, brød og en stor hat.
sentences.translation: Pippi buys apples, bread and a large hat.

words.grammar: købe (to buy) · køber (present) · købte (past) · købt (past participle) · regular weak verb
et brød · brødet (the bread) · brød (loaves, pl.) · brødene (the loaves) · unchanged in plural

sentences.text: En mand siger: "Piger bærer ikke så store hatte!"
sentences.translation: A man says: 'Girls don't wear such large hats!'

words.grammar: bære (to carry/wear) · bærer (present) · bar (past) · båret (past participle) · strong verb
en hat · hatten (the hat) · hatte (hats) · hattene (the hats)

sentences.text: Pippi griner og siger: "Denne pige gør det!"
sentences.translation: Pippi laughs and says: 'This girl does!'

words.grammar: grine (to laugh/grin) · griner (present) · grinede (past) · grint (past participle) · regular weak verb
denne (common gender) · dette (neuter) · disse (plural) · demonstrative pronoun · denne her = this one here (emphatic)

sentences.text: Alle på markedet griner med.
sentences.translation: Everyone at the market laughs along.

words.grammar: alle (pl.) · al (common sg.) · alt (neuter sg.) · alle sammen = everyone together · alle andre = everyone else

sentences.text: Pippi giver æbler til børnene.
sentences.translation: Pippi gives apples to the children.

words.grammar: give (to give) · giver (present) · gav (past) · givet (past participle) · strong verb · give nogen noget = to give someone something
et barn · barnet (the child) · børn (children) · børnene (the children) · irregular plural: barn – børn

sentences.text: De er glade og takker hende.
sentences.translation: They are happy and thank her.

words.grammar: glad (sg.) · glade (pl./definite) · meget glad = very happy · glad for noget = happy about something
takke (to thank) · takker (present) · takkede (past) · Tak! = Thank you! · mange tak = thank you very much

sentences.text: Pippi går hjem med den store hat på og en tom kurv.
sentences.translation: Pippi walks home with the large hat on and an empty basket.

words.grammar: tom (common) · tomt (neuter) · tomme (pl./definite) · en tom kurv = an empty basket · opposite: fuld (full)
hjem = home (motion toward) · hjemme = at home (location) · gå hjem = to go home · Jeg er hjemme = I am at home

sentences.text: Det regner udenfor.
sentences.translation: It is raining outside.

words.grammar: regne (to rain) · det regner = it is raining · regnede (past) · regnet (past participle) · impersonal verb – always used with 'det'
udenfor = outside · opposite: indenfor (inside) · can also be a preposition: udenfor huset = outside the house

sentences.text: Tommy og Annika keder sig derhjemme.
sentences.translation: Tommy and Annika are bored at home.

words.grammar: kede sig (to be bored) · reflexive verb · keder sig (present) · kedede sig (past) · Jeg keder mig = I am bored
derhjemme = at home (referring to their place) · hjemme = at home (general) · her hjemme = here at home

sentences.text: De løber over til Pippi.
sentences.translation: They run over to Pippi.

words.grammar: løbe (to run) · løber (present) · løb (past) · løbet (past participle) · strong verb
over til = over to · gå over til = to go over to · directional phrase indicating crossing a short distance

sentences.text: Pippi åbner døren og siger: "Kom ind!"
sentences.translation: Pippi opens the door and says: 'Come in!'

words.grammar: åbne (to open) · åbner (present) · åbnede (past) · åbnet (past participle) · opposite: lukke (to close)
en dør · døren (the door) · døre (doors) · dørene (the doors)

sentences.text: De sidder inde og hører på regnen.
sentences.translation: They sit inside and listen to the rain.

words.grammar: sidde (to sit) · sidder (present) · sad (past) · siddet (past participle) · strong verb
høre på (to listen to) · hører (present) · hørte (past) · distinct from lytte til (to listen attentively) · høre (to hear)

sentences.text: Pippi fortæller historier om sin far og havet.
sentences.translation: Pippi tells stories about her father and the sea.

words.grammar: fortælle (to tell/narrate) · fortæller (present) · fortalte (past) · fortalt (past participle) · irregular weak verb
et hav · havet (the sea) · have (seas) · have can also mean 'garden': en have · context distinguishes the two

sentences.text: Hendes far er kaptajn på et stort skib.
sentences.translation: Her father is a captain on a big ship.

words.grammar: en kaptajn · kaptajnen (the captain) · borrowed word from French/Dutch via English
et skib · skibet (the ship) · skibe (ships) · skibene (the ships)

sentences.text: Tommy siger: "Det lyder farligt!"
sentences.translation: Tommy says: 'That sounds dangerous!'

words.grammar: lyde (to sound) · det lyder godt = that sounds good · lød (past) · lydt (past participle) · strong verb
farlig (common) · farligt (neuter) · farlige (pl./definite) · en farlig situation = a dangerous situation

sentences.text: Pippi griner: "Nej! Det er et stort eventyr!"
sentences.translation: Pippi laughs: 'No! It is a great adventure!'

words.grammar: et eventyr · eventyret (the adventure/fairy tale) · eventyr (plural, unchanged) · H.C. Andersens eventyr = H.C. Andersen's fairy tales

sentences.text: Regnen stopper, og himlen bliver blå igen.
sentences.translation: The rain stops, and the sky becomes blue again.

words.grammar: en himmel · himlen (the sky) · the -el drops before -en suffix · himlen er blå = the sky is blue
stoppe (to stop) · stopper (present) · stoppede (past) · stoppe op = to come to a halt · loanword from English

sentences.text: De tre venner løber ud og leger.
sentences.translation: The three friends run outside and play.

words.grammar: lege (to play) · leger (present) · legede (past) · used for children's play · spille = to play sport/music/games
tre = three · used before both common and neuter nouns · tre venner = three friends

sentences.text: I Danmark er der mange grønne bakker.
sentences.translation: In Denmark there are many green hills.

words.grammar: en bakke · bakken (the hill) · bakker (hills) · bakkerne (the hills) · also means 'trays' (context-dependent)
grøn (common sg.) · grønt (neuter sg.) · grønne (pl./definite) · grøn is also used for 'eco-friendly'

sentences.text: Under bakkerne bor de underjordiske.
sentences.translation: Beneath the hills live the underground folk.

words.grammar: underjordisk (sg.) · underjordiske (pl./definite) · used as noun: de underjordiske = the hidden people · under + jord (earth) + isk
under = under/beneath · under bordet = under the table · can show location or motion · under natten = during the night

sentences.text: Om natten åbner højen sig.
sentences.translation: At night the hill opens itself.

words.grammar: en høj · højen (the hill/mound) · høje (hills) · elverhøj = elf hill · a culturally significant word in Danish folklore
om natten = at night (habitual) · i nat = tonight · natten (the night) · en nat · god nat = good night

sentences.text: Elverpigerne danser i det bløde græs.
sentences.translation: The elf maidens dance in the soft grass.

words.grammar: en elverpige · elverpigen (the elf maiden) · elverpiger (pl.) · compound: elver (elf) + pige (girl)
danse (to dance) · danser (present) · dansede (past) · danset (past participle) · regular weak verb
et græs · græsset (the grass) · no standard plural · slå græs = to mow the lawn

sentences.text: En ung mand ser lyset fra højen.
sentences.translation: A young man sees the light from the hill.

words.grammar: et lys · lyset (the light) · lys (lights, pl.) · tænde lyset = to turn on the light · also adj: lys = light/bright
ung (common) · ungt (neuter) · unge (pl./definite) · en ung mand = a young man · de unge = the young people

sentences.text: Han hører musik og føler sig underlig tiltrukket.
sentences.translation: He hears music and feels strangely drawn.

words.grammar: en musik · musikken (the music) · uncountable · spille musik = to play music
underlig (common) · underligt (neuter) · underlige (pl./definite) · en underlig følelse = a strange feeling

sentences.text: Han går nærmere og ser elverpigerne.
sentences.translation: He walks closer and sees the elf maidens.

words.grammar: nær (near) · nærmere (comparative) · nærmest (superlative) · komme nærmere = to come closer

sentences.text: De smiler og inviterer ham med i dansen.
sentences.translation: They smile and invite him to join the dance.

words.grammar: smile (to smile) · smiler (present) · smilede (past) · et smil (n.) = a smile
invitere (to invite) · inviterer (present) · inviterede (past) · en invitation (c.) = an invitation

sentences.text: Han danser hele natten og glemmer alt.
sentences.translation: He dances all night and forgets everything.

words.grammar: glemme (to forget) · glemmer (present) · glemte (past) · glemt (past participle) · irregular weak verb
hel (common/neuter sg.) · hele (definite/pl.) · hele natten = all night · hele verden = the whole world

sentences.text: Da solen kommer op, er elverpigerne væk.
sentences.translation: When the sun comes up, the elf maidens are gone.

words.grammar: en sol · solen (the sun) · sole (suns, rare) · solen skinner = the sun shines · solskin (n.) = sunshine
væk = gone/away · de er væk = they are gone · gå væk = go away · forsvinde = to disappear

sentences.text: Manden står alene på den grønne bakke.
sentences.translation: The man stands alone on the green hill.

words.grammar: stå (to stand) · står (present) · stod (past) · stået (past participle) · strong verb

sentences.text: Han husker dansen, men han ved ikke om det var en drøm.
sentences.translation: He remembers the dance, but he does not know if it was a dream.

words.grammar: huske (to remember) · husker (present) · huskede (past) · Kan du huske? = Do you remember?
en drøm · drømmen (the dream) · drømme (dreams) · drømme (to dream) – same form as the noun plural

sentences.text: Der var engang en prins.
sentences.translation: Once upon a time there was a prince.

words.grammar: være (to be) · var (past) · har været (perfect) · "Der var engang" = once upon a time, the fairy-tale opening formula
en prins · prinsen (the prince) · prinser (plural) · prinserne (the princes)

sentences.text: Han ville gerne have en rigtig prinsesse.
sentences.translation: He wanted to have a real princess.

words.grammar: ville (modal: want to/would) · present: vil · ville gerne = would like to · modal verb, no "at" before the following infinitive
rigtig (common) · rigtigt (neuter) · rigtige (pl./def.) · en rigtig prinsesse = a true (genuine) princess, not just any princess

sentences.text: Han rejste over hele verden, men han fandt ingen.
sentences.translation: He traveled all over the world, but he found none.

words.grammar: rejse (to travel) · rejste (past) · har rejst (perfect) · weak verb, regular -te ending
ingen (for en-words/persons) · intet (for et-words) · negative pronoun, replaces a noun + negation together

sentences.text: En aften kom der en prinsesse til slottet.
sentences.translation: One evening a princess came to the castle.

words.grammar: komme (to come) · kom (past) · er kommet (perfect) · strong verb, vowel change but no -te ending
et slot · slottet (the castle) · slotte (plural) · slottene (the castles)

sentences.text: Det regnede, og hun var meget våd.
sentences.translation: It was raining, and she was very wet.

words.grammar: regne (to rain) · regnede (past) · har regnet (perfect) · weak verb
våd (common) · vådt (neuter) · våde (pl./def.)

sentences.text: Dronningen sagde: "Vi skal teste hende."
sentences.translation: The queen said: "We must test her."

words.grammar: skulle (modal) · skal (present) · used for plans, decisions, and instructions · skal teste = is going to test
en dronning · dronningen (the queen) · dronninger (plural)

sentences.text: Dronningen lagde en ært under tyve madrasser.
sentences.translation: The queen put a pea under twenty mattresses.

words.grammar: tyve (20) · cardinal number · combines directly with plural noun, e.g. tyve madrasser
en ært · ærten (the pea) · ærter (plural)

sentences.text: Prinsessen sov på sengen om natten.
sentences.translation: The princess slept in the bed during the night.

words.grammar: sove (to sleep) · sov (past) · har sovet (perfect) · strong verb
en nat · natten (the night) · om natten = during the night

sentences.text: Om morgenen spurgte dronningen: "Hvordan sov du?"
sentences.translation: In the morning the queen asked: "How did you sleep?"

words.grammar: spørge (to ask) · spurgte (past) · har spurgt (perfect) · strong/irregular verb

sentences.text: Prinsessen sagde: "Jeg sov dårligt. Der var noget hårdt i sengen."
sentences.translation: The princess said: "I slept badly. There was something hard in the bed."

words.grammar: dårlig (bad, adj.) · dårligt (adverb / neuter form) · opposite of godt/godt
hård (common) · hårdt (neuter) · hårde (pl./def.) · agrees with "noget" (neuter)

sentences.text: Nu vidste de, at hun var en rigtig prinsesse.
sentences.translation: Now they knew that she was a real princess.

words.grammar: vide (to know - facts) · vidste (past) · har vidst (perfect) · irregular, do not confuse with "kende" (know a person/place)

sentences.text: Prinsen og prinsessen blev gift og levede lykkeligt.
sentences.translation: The prince and the princess got married and lived happily.

words.grammar: blive (to become) · blev (past) · er blevet (perfect) · blive gift = to get married
lykkelig (happy, adj.) · lykkeligt (adverb/neuter)

metadata.adaptedFrom: Adapted from H.C. Andersen (1835), simplified for A1 learners

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Der var engang en prins.
grammarPoints.explanation: This fixed phrase always opens a Danish eventyr, just like "once upon a time" in English. It uses the past tense of være (var) and the dummy word der.

grammarPoints.rule: Modal verb "skal" for plans and decisions
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Vi skal teste hende.
grammarPoints.explanation: Skal expresses an intention or instruction, not just future time. It signals "we are going to, as a decision" — stronger and more deliberate than just predicting what will happen.

grammarPoints.rule: Question word order: verb before subject
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hvordan sov du?
grammarPoints.explanation: After a question word (hvordan, hvad, hvor...), the verb comes immediately, then the subject. There is no extra "do/did" word like in English.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han fandt ingen.
grammarPoints.explanation: Ingen replaces an entire noun phrase plus its negation — "ingen prinsesse" becomes just "ingen". Use intet instead for et-words.

grammarPoints.rule: Numbers combine directly with plural nouns
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Dronningen lagde en ært under tyve madrasser.
grammarPoints.explanation: Danish needs no linking word between a number and the noun it counts: tyve madrasser, not "tyve af madrasser".

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Prinsen og prinsessen blev gift.
grammarPoints.explanation: Blive describes a change of state, contrasted with være, which describes an existing state. Blive gift = to get married; være gift = to be married.

sentences.text: Der boede engang tre brødre på en gård.
sentences.translation: Once there lived three brothers on a farm.

words.grammar: bo (to live, reside) · boede (past) · har boet (perfect) · weak verb
en gård · gården (the farm) · gårde (plural) · gårdene (the farms)

sentences.text: De to ældste brødre var meget kloge, men den yngste hed Klods-Hans.
sentences.translation: The two oldest brothers were very clever, but the youngest was called Klods-Hans.

words.grammar: klog (common) · klogt (neuter) · kloge (pl./def.) · superlative: klogest/klogeste
hedde (to be called/named) · hed (past) · har heddet (perfect) · irregular verb

sentences.text: Kongen havde en datter, og hun ville gerne giftes med en klog mand.
sentences.translation: The king had a daughter, and she wanted to marry a clever man.

words.grammar: en konge · kongen (the king) · konger (plural)
en datter · datteren (the daughter) · døtre (plural)

sentences.text: De to kloge brødre red til slottet på flotte heste.
sentences.translation: The two clever brothers rode to the castle on fine horses.

words.grammar: ride (to ride) · red (past) · har redet (perfect) · strong verb
flot (common) · flot (neuter, irregular - no -t added) · flotte (pl./def.)

sentences.text: Klods-Hans ville også af sted, så han satte sig på en gedebuk.
sentences.translation: Klods-Hans also wanted to go, so he sat himself on a billy goat.

words.grammar: så (result conjunction) · coordinating, like "og"/"men" · normal word order continues after it
sætte sig (to sit down, reflexive) · satte (past) · har sat sig (perfect) · weak verb + reflexive pronoun

sentences.text: På vejen fandt han en død krage, en gammel træsko og noget mudder.
sentences.translation: On the way he found a dead crow, an old wooden shoe, and some mud.

words.grammar: en krage · kragen (the crow) · krager (plural)
mudder (uncountable, no plural) · mudderet (the mud)

sentences.text: Han puttede alting i lommen, for det kunne måske bruges.
sentences.translation: He put everything in his pocket, because it could perhaps be used.

words.grammar: for (causal, coordinating) · comma before it · followed by normal (not V2-inverted) word order
bruge (to use) · bruges (passive -s form, present) · can be used

sentences.text: Da brødrene kom til prinsessen, kunne de ikke svare på hendes spørgsmål.
sentences.translation: When the brothers arrived at the princess, they couldn't answer her questions.

words.grammar: da (subordinating, only for past-time "when") · contrast with når (present/future "when")
kunne (modal: can/could) · kunne (past) · kunne ikke = couldn't

sentences.text: Klods-Hans svarede med kragen, træskoen og mudderet, og prinsessen lo højt.
sentences.translation: Klods-Hans answered with the crow, the wooden shoe, and the mud, and the princess laughed loudly.

words.grammar: svare (to answer) · svarede (past) · har svaret (perfect) · weak verb
le (to laugh) · lo (past) · har let (perfect) · strong verb

sentences.text: Hun sagde: "Han er sjov, og jeg vil gifte mig med ham!"
sentences.translation: She said: "He is funny, and I want to marry him!"

words.grammar: sjov (common) · sjovt (neuter) · sjove (pl./def.)
gifte sig med (to marry someone, reflexive) · gifter/giftede/har giftet · always reflexive + med

sentences.text: Kongen blev overrasket, men han holdt sit ord.
sentences.translation: The king was surprised, but he kept his word.

words.grammar: overrasket (common/neuter, same form) · past participle used as adjective · blev overrasket = was surprised
holde (to hold/keep) · holdt (past) · har holdt (perfect) · holde sit ord = to keep one's word

sentences.text: Klods-Hans og prinsessen blev gift, og han blev konge.
sentences.translation: Klods-Hans and the princess got married, and he became king.

words.grammar: en konge · kongen (the king) · konger (plural)

metadata.adaptedFrom: Adapted from H.C. Andersen (1855), simplified for A2 learners

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han ville også af sted, så han satte sig på en gedebuk.
grammarPoints.explanation: Så links a cause to its result. Like other coordinating conjunctions (og, men, for), it doesn't change the word order of the clause that follows it.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han puttede alting i lommen, for det kunne måske bruges.
grammarPoints.explanation: Both mean "because", but for keeps normal word order after the comma, while fordi (a subordinating conjunction) would push the verb to the end of its clause.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Da brødrene kom til prinsessen, kunne de ikke svare.
grammarPoints.explanation: Da means "when" but only for a single completed event in the past. Når is used instead for repeated past events, or present/future "when".

grammarPoints.rule: V2 inversion after a subordinate clause
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Da brødrene kom til prinsessen, kunne de ikke svare.
grammarPoints.explanation: When a sentence opens with a subordinate clause, the main clause verb must come immediately after the comma, before its own subject — Danish main clauses always keep the verb in second position overall.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: De kunne ikke svare på hendes spørgsmål.
grammarPoints.explanation: Negation with ikke comes right after the modal verb (kunne), not after the main verb (svare), which stays in its infinitive form at the end.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han holdt sit ord.
grammarPoints.explanation: Sit/sin/sine refer back to the subject of the sentence ("his own word"). Hans would mean the word belonged to a different male person mentioned elsewhere.

sentences.text: Der var engang en kejser, som elskede smukt tøj mere end alt andet.
sentences.translation: Once there was an emperor who loved beautiful clothes more than anything else.

words.grammar: en kejser · kejseren (the emperor) · kejsere (plural)
som (relative pronoun, subject or object) · invariable — same form for people and things

sentences.text: En dag kom der to bedragere til byen.
sentences.translation: One day two swindlers came to the town.

words.grammar: en bedrager · bedrageren (the swindler) · bedragere (plural) · bedragerne (the swindlers)

sentences.text: De sagde, at de kunne væve det smukkeste stof i verden.
sentences.translation: They said that they could weave the most beautiful fabric in the world.

words.grammar: væve (to weave) · væver (present) · vævede (past) · har vævet (perfect)
et stof · stoffet (the fabric) · stoffer (plural)

sentences.text: Stoffet var usynligt for alle dumme mennesker, sagde de.
sentences.translation: The fabric was invisible to all stupid people, they said.

words.grammar: usynlig (common) · usynligt (neuter) · usynlige (pl./def.) · u- = negative prefix, synlig = visible

sentences.text: Kejseren betalte dem meget guld og bad dem væve med det samme.
sentences.translation: The emperor paid them a lot of gold and asked them to weave at once.

words.grammar: bede (to ask/request) · bad (past) · har bedt (perfect) · bede nogen gøre noget = ask someone to do something (no "at")
guld (uncountable) · guldet (the gold)

sentences.text: Ministrene så ingenting, men de turde ikke sige det.
sentences.translation: The ministers saw nothing, but they didn't dare say it.

words.grammar: turde (modal: dare) · tør (present) · turde (past) · har turdet (perfect)
ingenting (nothing, emphatic) · stronger/more colloquial than "intet"

sentences.text: Alle var bange for at blive kaldt dumme, så de roste det smukke stof.
sentences.translation: Everyone was afraid of being called stupid, so they praised the beautiful fabric.

words.grammar: være bange for at + infinitive = to be afraid of (doing) something
rose (to praise) · roste (past) · har rost (perfect) · weak verb

sentences.text: Kejseren gik gennem byen i sit nye, usynlige tøj.
sentences.translation: The emperor walked through the town in his new, invisible clothes.

words.grammar: gennem (through) · governs accusative-like position · gå gennem byen = walk through the town

sentences.text: Folket klappede og råbte, fordi ingen turde sige sandheden.
sentences.translation: The people clapped and shouted, because no one dared tell the truth.

words.grammar: fordi (subordinating) · introduces a clause where the verb stays after the subject, but the clause can't stand alone
en sandhed · sandheden (the truth) · sandheder (plural)

sentences.text: Pludselig råbte et lille barn: "Men han har jo ikke noget tøj på!"
sentences.translation: Suddenly a small child shouted: "But he isn't wearing any clothes at all!"

words.grammar: jo (modal particle, untranslatable alone) · adds emphasis to something the speaker sees as self-evident
et barn · barnet (the child) · børn (plural, irregular) · børnene (the children)

sentences.text: Hviskingen bredte sig, og snart sagde alle det samme.
sentences.translation: The whisper spread, and soon everyone said the same thing.

words.grammar: brede sig (to spread, reflexive) · breder sig/bredte sig/har bredt sig · weak verb + reflexive pronoun

sentences.text: Kejseren blev rød i ansigtet, men han fortsatte processionen med rank ryg.
sentences.translation: The emperor turned red in the face, but he continued the procession with a straight back.

words.grammar: fortsætte (to continue) · fortsatte (past) · har fortsat (perfect) · weak verb

metadata.adaptedFrom: Adapted from H.C. Andersen (1837), simplified for upper-A2 learners

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: ...en kejser, som elskede smukt tøj...
grammarPoints.explanation: Som introduces a clause describing a noun, acting as the subject inside that clause. It's invariable — used for both people and things.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: De sagde, at de kunne væve det smukkeste stof i verden.
grammarPoints.explanation: At introduces what someone said as a clause rather than a direct quote. The word order inside stays subject-verb, just like a normal statement.

grammarPoints.rule: Bare infinitive after verbs of request: "bede nogen gøre noget"
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han bad dem væve med det samme.
grammarPoints.explanation: After bede (ask/request) + a person, the following verb is a bare infinitive with no at — unlike most other verb + infinitive constructions in Danish.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Folket klappede, fordi ingen turde sige sandheden.
grammarPoints.explanation: Fordi introduces a subordinate clause, which cannot stand alone as a full sentence. Compare with the coordinating for from story 009, which can.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han har jo ikke noget tøj på!
grammarPoints.explanation: Jo has no direct English translation. It marks the statement as something the speaker believes is — or should be — obvious or already known to the listener.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Kejseren blev rød i ansigtet.
grammarPoints.explanation: The blive + adjective pattern for change of state appears again here, reinforcing the construction first introduced with blev gift in story 008.

sentences.text: Dybt under Kronborg Slot ligger en kæmpe kriger, som hedder Holger Danske.
sentences.translation: Deep under Kronborg Castle lies a giant warrior, who is called Holger Danske.

words.grammar: en kæmpe (a giant, noun) · kæmpe- (giant-, used as a prefix/adj.) · kæmpestor = gigantic
en kriger · krigeren (the warrior) · krigere (plural)

sentences.text: Han har siddet der i mange hundrede år og sover tungt.
sentences.translation: He has been sitting there for many hundred years and sleeps heavily.

words.grammar: sidde (to sit) · sad (past) · har siddet (perfect) · present perfect = action started in the past, continuing now

sentences.text: Hans skæg er vokset langt ned og har viklet sig om bordet foran ham.
sentences.translation: His beard has grown long down and has wound itself around the table in front of him.

words.grammar: vokse (to grow) · voksede (past) · er vokset (perfect, takes "er" not "har") · motion/change-of-state verb
et skæg · skægget (the beard) · skæg (plural, unchanged)

sentences.text: Ifølge legenden vil Holger Danske vågne, hvis Danmark nogensinde er i stor fare.
sentences.translation: According to the legend, Holger Danske will wake up if Denmark is ever in great danger.

words.grammar: ifølge + noun = according to · ifølge legenden = according to the legend
hvis (conditional conjunction) · introduces a condition · followed by present tense for future possibility

sentences.text: Da han var ung, kæmpede han modigt for kongen og for landet.
sentences.translation: When he was young, he fought bravely for the king and for the country.

words.grammar: kæmpe (to fight) · kæmpede (past) · har kæmpet (perfect) · weak verb
modig (brave, adj.) · modigt (adverb/neuter form)

sentences.text: Selvom han nu sover, holder han stadig sit sværd fast i hånden.
sentences.translation: Even though he now sleeps, he still holds his sword firmly in his hand.

words.grammar: selvom (subordinating, concessive) · admits one fact while contrasting with another
et sværd · sværdet (the sword) · sværd (plural, unchanged)

sentences.text: Mange børn har hørt historien om ham, når de besøgte slottet om sommeren.
sentences.translation: Many children have heard the story about him, when they visited the castle in the summer.

words.grammar: når (subordinating) · used for repeated or habitual events, in any tense — contrast with da for a single past event

sentences.text: Nogle siger, at man kan høre ham snorke dybt nede i kælderen.
sentences.translation: Some say that you can hear him snore deep down in the cellar.

words.grammar: man (impersonal/generic subject pronoun) · used where English often says "you" or "people"
en kælder · kælderen (the cellar) · kældre (plural)

sentences.text: Under Anden Verdenskrig brugte den danske modstandsbevægelse hans navn som symbol på frihed.
sentences.translation: During the Second World War, the Danish resistance movement used his name as a symbol of freedom.

words.grammar: en modstandsbevægelse · modstandsbevægelsen (the resistance movement) · compound: modstand (resistance) + bevægelse (movement)
en frihed · friheden (the freedom)

sentences.text: Statuen af Holger Danske, som man kan se i kælderen i dag, blev lavet af billedhuggeren H.P. Pedersen-Dan.
sentences.translation: The statue of Holger Danske, which one can see in the cellar today, was made by the sculptor H.P. Pedersen-Dan.

words.grammar: som (relative pronoun, here as object of "se") · can often be dropped in casual speech when used as object, but kept here
blive lavet af (passive: to be made by) · blive + past participle + af (agent) = standard Danish passive

sentences.text: Hvis du går ned ad de mørke trapper, kan du selv møde den sovende kæmpe.
sentences.translation: If you go down the dark stairs, you can meet the sleeping giant yourself.

words.grammar: sove (to sleep) → sovende (present participle/adjective) · -ende ending, like English -ing
en trappe · trappen (the stairs) · trapper (plural)

sentences.text: Indtil da venter Holger Danske tålmodigt, klar til at forsvare Danmark igen.
sentences.translation: Until then, Holger Danske waits patiently, ready to defend Danmark again.

words.grammar: tålmodig (patient, adj.) · tålmodigt (adverb/neuter form)
forsvare (to defend) · forsvarer (present) · forsvarede (past) · har forsvaret (perfect)

metadata.adaptedFrom: Danish national legend (Ogier the Dane), adapted for upper-A2/B1 learners

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Holger Danske vil vågne, hvis Danmark nogensinde er i stor fare.
grammarPoints.explanation: Hvis introduces a condition. Danish typically uses present tense even when describing a future possibility — there's no separate future conditional form needed.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Selvom han nu sover, holder han stadig sit sværd fast.
grammarPoints.explanation: Selvom admits one fact while contrasting it with something unexpected. The subordinate clause can come first, with the main clause verb following directly after the comma.

grammarPoints.rule: Present perfect for ongoing past-to-present states
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Han har siddet der i mange hundrede år.
grammarPoints.explanation: Har/er + past participle describes something that started in the past and is still true now — distinct from simple past (sad), which would imply the sitting is finished.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Statuen blev lavet af billedhuggeren H.P. Pedersen-Dan.
grammarPoints.explanation: This is the standard way to form the passive in spoken/everyday Danish: blive + past participle, with af introducing who performed the action.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Da han var ung... / ...når de besøgte slottet om sommeren.
grammarPoints.explanation: Da is for a single, completed event in the past. Når is for repeated or habitual events (in any tense) and for present/future "when".

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Ifølge legenden vil Holger Danske vågne...
grammarPoints.explanation: Ifølge is a simple preposition for citing a source — legenden, eksperterne, avisen — followed directly by the noun, no extra connecting word needed.

sentences.text: I Kina boede der engang en kejser, hvis palads var det smukkeste i hele verden.
sentences.translation: In China there once lived an emperor, whose palace was the most beautiful in the whole world.

words.grammar: hvis (possessive relative pronoun) · different word from hvis = "if" — context distinguishes them
et palads · paladset (the palace) · paladser (plural)

sentences.text: I skoven uden for paladset boede en lille, grå nattergal, som sang så smukt, at folk fra hele landet kom for at lytte.
sentences.translation: In the forest outside the palace lived a small, grey nightingale, which sang so beautifully that people from the whole country came to listen.

words.grammar: en nattergal · nattergalen (the nightingale) · nattergale (plural)
så + adjective/adverb...at + clause = result construction, expressing degree and consequence

sentences.text: Da kejseren endelig hørte fuglens sang, blev han dybt bevæget og bad om, at den skulle bo ved hoffet.
sentences.translation: When the emperor finally heard the bird's song, he was deeply moved and asked that it should live at the court.

words.grammar: en fugl · fuglen (the bird) · fuglens (the bird's, genitive) · -s added directly to the definite form
bevæget (common/neuter, same form) · past participle of bevæge, used as adjective · dybt bevæget = deeply moved

sentences.text: En dag fik kejseren en kunstig nattergal i gave, lavet af guld og dækket med ædelstene.
sentences.translation: One day the emperor received an artificial nightingale as a gift, made of gold and covered with precious stones.

words.grammar: kunstig (common) · kunstigt (neuter) · kunstige (pl./def.) · opposite of "ægte" (real/genuine)
en ædelsten · ædelstenen (the precious stone) · ædelstene (plural) · compound: ædel (noble) + sten (stone)

sentences.text: Den mekaniske fugl kunne kun synge én sang, men hoffet foretrak den, fordi den glimtede så flot.
sentences.translation: The mechanical bird could only sing one song, but the court preferred it, because it glittered so finely.

words.grammar: foretrække (to prefer) · foretrak (past) · har foretrukket (perfect) · strong/irregular verb, takes direct object
glimte (to glitter/sparkle) · glimtede (past) · har glimtet (perfect) · weak verb

sentences.text: Imens forsvandt den rigtige nattergal tilbage til skoven, ked af at være glemt.
sentences.translation: Meanwhile, the real nightingale disappeared back to the forest, sad about being forgotten.

words.grammar: imens (while/meanwhile) · can open a sentence as a time connector between two parallel events
forsvinde (to disappear) · forsvandt (past) · er forsvundet (perfect) · strong verb

sentences.text: Mange år senere blev kejseren alvorligt syg, og Døden satte sig på hans bryst.
sentences.translation: Many years later the emperor became seriously ill, and Death sat down on his chest.

words.grammar: alvorlig (serious, adj.) · alvorligt (adverb/neuter form)
et bryst · brystet (the chest) · bryster (plural)

sentences.text: Den kunstige fugl kunne ikke synge, for ingen havde trukket dens fjeder op.
sentences.translation: The artificial bird could not sing, because no one had wound up its spring.

words.grammar: trække op (to wind up) · trak op (past) · havde trukket op (pluperfect) · action completed before another past moment
dens (possessive for en-words/common gender, non-personal) · dets (for et-words)

sentences.text: Pludselig hørtes en sang udenfor vinduet: den rigtige nattergal var vendt tilbage.
sentences.translation: Suddenly a song was heard outside the window: the real nightingale had returned.

words.grammar: høre (to hear) · hørtes (passive -s form, past) · alternative to "blev hørt"
vende tilbage (to return) · vendte tilbage (past) · var vendt tilbage (pluperfect, takes "var" not "havde")

sentences.text: Den sang om kirkegårde, hvor man kan hvile, og om livet, som er værd at leve.
sentences.translation: It sang about churchyards, where one can rest, and about life, which is worth living.

words.grammar: hvor (relative adverb, place) · introduces a clause describing a location
værd (invariable) · være værd at + infinitive = to be worth doing

sentences.text: Døden blev bevæget af sangen og forsvandt langsomt ud i den kolde nat.
sentences.translation: Death was moved by the song and slowly disappeared out into the cold night.

words.grammar: kold (common) · koldt (neuter) · kolde (pl./def.)

sentences.text: Kejseren blev rask igen, og fra da af bad han altid nattergalen om at blive hos ham som ven, ikke som fange.
sentences.translation: The emperor became well again, and from then on he always asked the nightingale to stay with him as a friend, not as a prisoner.

words.grammar: fra da af (fixed time expression) · marks a lasting change starting at a specific past moment
en fange · fangen (the prisoner) · fanger (plural)

metadata.adaptedFrom: Adapted from H.C. Andersen (1843), simplified for B1 learners

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: ...en kejser, hvis palads var det smukkeste i hele verden.
grammarPoints.explanation: Hvis as a relative pronoun means "whose" and links a noun back to something it possesses. It looks identical to the conditional hvis ("if") but is always followed by a noun, not a full new clause.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: ...en nattergal, som sang så smukt, at folk fra hele landet kom for at lytte.
grammarPoints.explanation: Så + adjective/adverb sets up a degree, and at + clause delivers the consequence — together they express "so [X] that [Y happened]".

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Ingen havde trukket dens fjeder op. / Den rigtige nattergal var vendt tilbage.
grammarPoints.explanation: Har/havde or er/var (depending on the verb) + past participle marks an action completed before another point already in the past — one step further back than simple past.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Døden blev bevæget af sangen.
grammarPoints.explanation: This set's recurring blive pattern now combines with af to show passive cause: "became moved by" — emotional change of state triggered by something external.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Den sang om kirkegårde, hvor man kan hvile, og om livet, som er værd at leve.
grammarPoints.explanation: Two separate relative clauses, each attached to its own noun (kirkegårde / livet), can sit side by side in one sentence — common in more literary Danish.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Fra da af bad han altid nattergalen om at blive hos ham.
grammarPoints.explanation: This phrase means "from that point onward" and is used to mark a lasting change after a turning-point moment in a story.

sentences.text: Dybt under havet, hvor vandet er blåere end himlen, lå havkongens smukke palads.
sentences.translation: Deep under the sea, where the water is bluer than the sky, lay the sea king's beautiful palace.

words.grammar: blå (common/neuter, same form) · blåere (comparative) · blåest/blåeste (superlative) · blåere end = bluer than
en havkonge · havkongen (the sea king) · havkongens (genitive: the sea king's) · compound: hav (sea) + konge (king)

sentences.text: Hans yngste datter, den lille havfrue, drømte altid om verden over havets overflade.
sentences.translation: His youngest daughter, the little mermaid, always dreamed about the world above the sea's surface.

words.grammar: en havfrue · havfruen (the mermaid) · havfruer (plural) · compound: hav (sea) + frue (lady)
en overflade · overfladen (the surface) · compound: over (above) + flade (flat surface)

sentences.text: På sin femtende fødselsdag fik hun endelig lov til at svømme op og se solen for første gang.
sentences.translation: On her fifteenth birthday she was finally allowed to swim up and see the sun for the first time.

words.grammar: få lov til at + infinitive = to be allowed/given permission to do something
en sol · solen (the sun)

sentences.text: Hun så et skib, hvorpå en ung prins dansede og lo sammen med sine venner.
sentences.translation: She saw a ship, on which a young prince danced and laughed together with his friends.

words.grammar: hvorpå = hvor + på, formal relative adverb · written-register alternative to "som ... på"
sin/sit/sine (reflexive possessive, plural form) · refers back to the subject (prinsen) — "his own friends"

sentences.text: Pludselig brød en voldsom storm løs, og skibet sank ned i de mørke bølger.
sentences.translation: Suddenly a violent storm broke loose, and the ship sank down into the dark waves.

words.grammar: en storm · stormen (the storm) · storme (plural)
synke (to sink) · sank (past) · er sunket (perfect) · strong verb

sentences.text: Den lille havfrue reddede prinsen og bar ham forsigtigt op på en sandstrand.
sentences.translation: The little mermaid saved the prince and carried him carefully up onto a sandy beach.

words.grammar: bære (to carry) · bar (past) · har båret (perfect) · strong verb
forsigtig (careful, adj.) · forsigtigt (adverb/neuter form)

sentences.text: Fra det øjeblik kunne hun ikke holde op med at tænke på ham, selvom han aldrig fik hendes ansigt at se.
sentences.translation: From that moment she couldn't stop thinking about him, even though he never got to see her face.

words.grammar: holde op med at + infinitive = to stop doing something · fixed phrase, always with "med"
et øjeblik · øjeblikket (the moment) · compound: øje (eye) + blik (glance) — literally "the blink of an eye"

sentences.text: For at blive menneske opsøgte hun havheksen, som boede mellem mørke, klæbrige tangskove.
sentences.translation: To become human she sought out the sea witch, who lived among dark, sticky kelp forests.

words.grammar: for at + infinitive = purpose clause ("in order to") · stronger than plain "at", which has no purpose meaning
opsøge (to seek out, visit deliberately) · opsøgte (past) · har opsøgt (perfect) · weak verb

sentences.text: Heksen tilbød hende et par ben, men kun hvis hun gav afkald på sin smukke stemme.
sentences.translation: The witch offered her a pair of legs, but only if she gave up her beautiful voice.

words.grammar: et ben · benet (the leg) · ben (plural, unchanged)
en stemme · stemmen (the voice) · stemmer (plural)

sentences.text: Hvert skridt, hun tog på land, føltes, som om hun trådte på skarpe knive.
sentences.translation: Every step she took on land felt as if she stepped on sharp knives.

words.grammar: som om + clause = as if, introduces a hypothetical/comparative clause describing how something feels or seems
et skridt · skridtet (the step) · skridt (plural, unchanged)

sentences.text: Selvom hun led i stilhed, kunne hun aldrig fortælle prinsen, at det var hende, der havde reddet ham.
sentences.translation: Even though she suffered in silence, she could never tell the prince that it was she who had saved him.

words.grammar: lide (to suffer) · led (past) · har lidt (perfect) · strong verb
cleft construction: det var X, der/som ... = emphasizes X as the one who did something

sentences.text: Til sidst valgte hun at ofre sig selv frem for at skade den mand, hun elskede så højt.
sentences.translation: In the end she chose to sacrifice herself rather than harm the man she loved so dearly.

words.grammar: frem for at + infinitive = rather than (doing something), contrasts two possible actions
ofre sig selv (to sacrifice oneself, reflexive) · ofrer/ofrede/har ofret + sig selv

metadata.adaptedFrom: Adapted excerpt from H.C. Andersen (1837), simplified for B1 learners

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: ...hvor vandet er blåere end himlen...
grammarPoints.explanation: Most Danish adjectives form the comparative by adding -ere (blå → blåere), paired with end ("than") to complete the comparison.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hun så et skib, hvorpå en ung prins dansede.
grammarPoints.explanation: Written Danish often fuses hvor + a preposition into one word (hvorpå = on which, hvori = in which). Spoken Danish usually prefers som ... på instead.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: For at blive menneske opsøgte hun havheksen.
grammarPoints.explanation: For at + infinitive expresses purpose ("in order to"), distinguishing it from plain at, which has no purpose meaning on its own.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hvert skridt føltes, som om hun trådte på skarpe knive.
grammarPoints.explanation: Som om introduces a comparison to something not literally true — describing how something feels or seems, rather than what actually happened.

grammarPoints.rule: Cleft sentences for emphasis: "det var X, der/som..."
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Det var hende, der havde reddet ham.
grammarPoints.explanation: This construction splits a sentence to spotlight one element (hende) as the answer to an implied question — "who saved him? It was she."

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hun valgte at ofre sig selv frem for at skade den mand, hun elskede.
grammarPoints.explanation: Frem for at + infinitive sets up a contrast between two possible actions, with the first one chosen over the second.
